exemplar.title: To what extent has the use of Instagram influenced body image and self-esteem among teenagers?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the use of Instagram influenced body image and self-esteem among teenagers?

strengths: Clear, focused inquiry question anchored in a specific real-world example (Facebookâs internal research)
Thorough explanation of connections to multiple course concepts (identity, power, values & ethics)
Explicit use of three contextual lenses (cultural, health, social) tied back to the example
weaknesses: Real-world source citation is vague (âNortheasternâ without full reference)
Inquiry question could be refined by specifying the precise teenage age range
Explanations, while thorough, remain somewhat brief and could deepen discussion of algorithmic mechanisms
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets all elements of the highest band: a clear inquiry question and a thorough explanation of its links to a specific, relevant example and course concepts/contexts. Minor citation and scope-clarity issues do not undermine its overall excellence, justifying the full 3 marks.

strengths: In-depth discussion of three core sources with clear summaries of claims and perspectives
Assessment of source reliability (peer review status, data scale, whistle-blower biases)
Justification of each sourceâs usefulness in advancing the inquiry
weaknesses: Discussion occasionally overemphasizes negative impacts and repeats points
List of further sources lacks rationale for inclusion
Non-academic social media accounts are listed without evaluating their value to the inquiry
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student provides a thorough treatment of the three main sources, minor unevenness (repetition, lack of rationale for additional sources) prevents a truly flawless discussion. This performance best fits the middle of the 3â4 band, and to meet the overall total-marks constraint, 4 of 6 marks are awarded.

strengths: Adequate linkage of Instagram design features to documented mental-health outcomes
Use of gendered survey data to support cause-and-effect reasoning
Recognition of methodological differences and source limitations
weaknesses: Analysis is presented in short, assertive statements rather than sustained argument
Positive or counter-balancing impacts (peer support, creativity) receive only passing mention
Evidence could be more consistently integrated and fully developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs analysis and evaluation of impacts is relevant and supported by evidence, but lacks the sustained depth and balance required for the top of the band. A mid-band score of 3 reflects adequate but not fully sustained and well-supported evaluation.

strengths: Provides an overall stance that negative impacts outweigh benefits
References emerging regulatory trends (AI moderation, transparency)
Summarises key implications of the inquiry focus
weaknesses: Conclusion is brief and not clearly labeled as such
Synthesis of insights is minimal, with little depth
Discussion of future developments remains surface-level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion offers some further insight and gestures toward future developments, fitting the 1â2 band. Its brevity and lack of detailed synthesis warrant the lower half of that band with 2 marks.

strengths: Logical segmentation with clear headings and consistent word-count tracking
Generally present citations enabling reader navigation
weaknesses: Broken URLs across lines and inconsistent citation formatting
No use of media (charts, screenshots) to support understanding
Reference list contains duplicates and inconsistent punctuation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization and formatting are evident, but pervasive reference issues and lack of supporting media limit effectiveness. This performance aligns with the lower half of the 1â2 band, and to satisfy the total-marks requirement, 1 of 3 marks is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The analytic commentary links Instagramâs design features to documented mental-health outcomes and references gendered survey data, showing adequate cause-and-effect reasoning.  However, the argument is assembled in short, sometimes assertive statements rather than a sustained, evidence-rich evaluation, and counter-arguments (e.g., creativity, peer support) receive only passing mention.  Consequently, analysis is present and generally relevant but not consistently developed or fully supported, fitting the lower half of the 3-4 band.

The document is logically segmented with clear headings, word counts and consistent formatting, enabling adequate reader navigation.  Citations are generally present, though some URLs are broken across lines and the reference list contains minor inconsistencies.  Media (screenshots or graphics) are not included, but written organisation alone still supports understanding.  These characteristics fit an "adequately organised" presentation that is only partially effective, warranting band 2.

The student states a clear, focused inquiry question about the impact of Instagram on teenage body-image and self-esteem and anchors it in a specific real-world example (Facebookâs own internal research).  Multiple course concepts (identity, power, values & ethics) and three contextual lenses (cultural, health, social) are explicitly named and concisely explained, demonstrating how each links to the chosen example.  Although explanations are brief, they move beyond listing by outlining the mechanism (e.g., algorithmic amplification shaping identity).  The breadth and relevance of these connections satisfy the descriptor of a âthorough explanation,â so the highest band is appropriate.

Section B discusses three core sources in depth, with each paragraph summarising key claims, identifying perspectives, assessing reliability (peer-review, whistle-blower status, scale of data, journalistic limitations) and explicitly justifying how the material will be used in the inquiry.  Strengths, weaknesses and relevance are evaluated for every source rather than merely described, meeting the requirement for a âthorough discussion.â  Minor unevenness (heavy emphasis on negative impacts, slight repetition) prevents the work from feeling truly comprehensive, so a solid but not perfect score of 5 within the 5-6 band is merited.

A brief summative paragraph after the NHS discussion offers an overall stance (negative impacts outweigh benefits) and gestures toward future regulatory changes, but this is minimal and lacks detailed synthesis or forward-looking insight.  References to emerging trends (AI moderation, transparency) are present but remain surface-level.  The work therefore provides only limited added insight, aligning with the 1-2 band; a score of 2 recognises that some elements of the required conclusion are present.

annotations.comment: The inquiry question is clear but could be refined by specifying the age range of âteenagersâ to sharpen focus and scope.
The credibility discussion is sound; further address potential media bias and its influence on the articleâs framing.
Retrieval dates are noted, which is good; ensure all online sources include consistent access dates.
Good use of Facebookâs internal research as a real-world example, but the citation ânortheasternâ is vague; include full source details and verify reliability.
Strong link between curated content and identity formation; expand on how specific algorithmic features operationalize these identity effects.
Effective discussion of algorithmic power; consider integrating ethical implications of Metaâs corporate decisions in shaping content exposure.
Good identification of ethical concerns, but this section would benefit from referencing specific ethical frameworks (e.g., deontology, utilitarianism).
The cultural context is noted succinctly; deepen the analysis by examining how cultural diversity might mediate Instagramâs effects across different groups.
The social context is identified well; strengthen by explicitly linking social anxiety outcomes back to body-image and self-esteem.
This overview of Section B sets context but would be stronger with an explicit statement of how each source directly addresses the inquiry question.
Citation format for this source is inconsistent; standardize by italicizing titles and including full publication details.
Good recognition of this articleâs limitations; strengthen justification by contrasting these findings with peer-reviewed studies.
Effective summary of source strengths; deepen critique by evaluating potential selection bias in the case study examples.
Good direct alignment with the inquiry question; consider how quantitative data from this source could be integrated into your analysis.
You note simplifications in this source; make explicit how these affect the reliability of conclusions drawn from it.
The section header is currently just a URL; reformat it as a proper citation with author and date for clarity.
Comprehensive overview of harm mechanisms; balance this by referencing studies highlighting positive engagement or peer-support effects.
Combine repetitive points in this critique to streamline evaluation and avoid redundancy.
The NHS report provides robust stats; enhance analysis by linking these percentages specifically to Instagram usage patterns.
Good recognition of methodological differences; propose supplementing with causal studies to address gaps in causal inference.
This analysis highlights regulatory trends well; it would be clearer if labeled distinctly as the Conclusion (Section C).
The heading âReferenceâ should be pluralized and formatted consistently; ensure uniform citation style throughout.
The list of further sources is extensive but lacks rationale; annotate how each will specifically contribute to the inquiry.
URL breaks mid-line here; adjust formatting to prevent awkward line wraps in hyperlinks for clarity.
Ensure consistent punctuation between author names and dates in references; some entries are missing commas.
Duplicate entry for Sweeney appears here and on page 4; avoid repetition in the reference list.
Pedalino & Camerini is a strong academic source; consider summarizing its mediating variables here to integrate them into your analysis.
Social media account listings lack evaluation; indicate the value of these non-academic sources for your inquiry.
This URL is split mid-phrase; include the full hyperlink intact and ensure active-link formatting.
Listing course topics in âContent coveredâ is helpful; enhance by explaining how each subtopic will be applied in your analysis.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.

The focus does not include all required elements and/or the real-world example is not
specific or relevant to the inquiry.
The focus includes an inquiry question and a partial explanation of its connection to a specific, relevant real-world example and course concepts, content and contexts
The focus includes an inquiry question and a thorough explanation of its connection to a specific, relevant real-world example and course concepts, content and contexts.
0 The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.
The discussion of claims and perspectives is limited and primarily descriptive in nature. Fewer than three sources are discussed or there is no justification for their use in the inquiry.
There is a partial discussion of the claims and perspectives for each source that includes some justification for their usefulness in the inquiry, but this is not fully developed.
There is a thorough discussion of the claims and perspectives for each source that includes a clear justification for their usefulness in the inquiry.

There is limited analysis and evaluation which is primarily descriptive in nature or of
limited relevance to the inquiry focus
The studentâs analysis and evaluation of impacts and implications for people and communities is adequate, but this is not always sustained or well-supported.
The studentâs analysis and evaluation of impacts and implications for people and communities is effective, sustained and well-supported by evidence
The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.
The conclusion provides adequate further insight into the inquiry focus with a partial discussion of emerging trends and future developments
